title: Vietnam Veterans Liver Fluke Cancer Study Act
short_title: Vietnam Veterans Liver Fluke Cancer Study Act
plain_summary: This bill requires the Department of Veterans Affairs to study bile duct cancer in veterans who served in Vietnam during the Vietnam War. The VA must find out how many Vietnam veterans get this type of cancer compared to other Americans. They also have to keep track of bile duct cancer cases using their cancer database and report what they find to Congress on a regular basis. The bill also gives the VA one extra month to meet a deadline about veteran pension payment limits.
last_action_text: Received in the Senate and Read twice and referred to the Committee on Veterans' Affairs.

sections.title: Section 1. Short Title
sections.text: This law can be called the Vietnam Veterans Liver Fluke Cancer Study Act.

sections.title: Section 2. Study on the Prevalence of Cholangiocarcinoma in Veterans Who Served in the Vietnam Theater of Operations During the Vietnam Era
sections.text: The Secretary of Veterans Affairs must work with the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention to study a type of cancer called cholangiocarcinoma (bile duct cancer) in Vietnam War veterans. This study must start within one hundred twenty days after this law is signed. The study will use information from two cancer tracking systems: the Veterans Affairs Central Cancer Registry and the National Program of Cancer Registries. The study must find out how often this cancer happens in Vietnam War veterans compared to all Americans from the start of the Vietnam War until this law is signed. The study must also look at who gets this cancer by age, gender, race, ethnicity, and where they lived when diagnosed. Within one year after finishing the study, the Secretary must send Congress a report with the results and suggestions for what should be done about any problems found. The Secretary must also send follow-up reports to Congress from time to time with updates. The Secretary must continue tracking this cancer in Vietnam War veterans using the Veterans Affairs cancer registry and include this information in the follow-up reports. In this section, Vietnam era means the time period defined in section one hundred one of title thirty-eight of the United States Code. Covered veterans of the Vietnam era means veterans who served in the Vietnam area during the Vietnam War.

sections.title: Section 3. Extension of Certain Limitation of Pension Payments
sections.text: This section changes a date in the law about veteran pension payments. It changes the date from November thirtieth, two thousand thirty-one to December thirty-first, two thousand thirty-one in section fifty-five oh three of title thirty-eight of the United States Code.
